name: First Home Alliance
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counseling agency providing free financial literacy classes, homebuyer education, and foreclosure prevention counseling to low-to-moderate income families across Maryland, Virginia, DC, and Illinois.
description_long:
First Home Alliance is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit housing counseling agency founded in 2002 by Mr. Laws, a Gulf War veteran and retired Army Master Sergeant. The organization was established to improve financial literacy, homeownership opportunities, and quality of life among underserved low-to-moderate income and military veteran communities. Since inception, the agency has served over 18,000 homebuyers, homeowners, and renters. The organization holds HUD-approved status, which certifies it meets federal standards for housing counseling services.

The agency offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services including virtual financial literacy classes every Thursday via Zoom and Facebook Live, first-time homebuyer education classes, virtual housing counseling by phone, credit counseling, mortgage payment assistance guidance, foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation counseling, rental counseling, and specialized programs like their SPARC program. All services are designed to help consumers understand loan options, make informed financial decisions, and achieve sustainable homeownership.

First Home Alliance distinguishes itself through its focus on military veterans and underserved communities, its HUD-approved certification, and its multi-state service area (Maryland, Virginia, DC, Illinois). The organization maintains an experienced staff of trained counselors and offers accessibility through multiple channels including virtual classes, phone counseling, and event-based workshops. Leadership is actively involved in community organizations including Veterans Associations of Real Estate Professionals and the Virginia Housing Homeownership Advisory Council.

As a legitimate nonprofit organization, First Home Alliance genuinely operates as a free-help resource with no financial product sales or predatory lending. The honest caveat is that their services are educational and advisory in nature—they help consumers understand options and navigate the homeownership process but do not directly provide loans or credit repair. Effectiveness depends on consumer engagement and follow-through with recommendations.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency meeting federal certification standards
Completely free virtual financial literacy classes every Thursday on Zoom and Facebook Live
Phone-based virtual housing counseling with no stated fees or costs
Specialized focus on military veterans and low-to-moderate income communities
Multi-state service area covering Maryland, Virginia, DC, and Illinois
Founded and led by military veteran with 20+ years serving 18,000+ families
Comprehensive services including first-time homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, and rental counseling
cons: Primarily advisory and educational—cannot directly provide loans or immediately resolve credit issues
Services depend on consumer follow-through; counseling alone does not guarantee loan approval
Limited geographic service area despite multi-state presence; not available nationwide
Virtual-first model may create accessibility barriers for those without reliable internet or phone access
No information on average counseling wait times or appointment availability
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking education on loan options and the homeownership process
Low-to-moderate income families at risk of foreclosure or needing loss mitigation assistance
Military veterans looking for housing counseling combined with veteran-community support
Renters seeking guidance on transitioning to homeownership or understanding housing rights
diagnosis: Best for first-time homebuyers and at-risk homeowners seeking free, unbiased education and counseling from a certified nonprofit. Primary caveat: these are advisory services—counseling improves decision-making and options but does not guarantee loan approval, credit repair, or prevent foreclosure without borrower action and lender cooperation.
services: Virtual financial literacy classes (free, weekly on Zoom and Facebook Live)
Credit counseling and credit counseling registration
Mortgage payment assistance guidance and support
Foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation counseling
SPARC program (specific details not listed but available)
FirstHome Dream program (specific details not listed but available)
In-person and virtual workshops and training events
Loan option explanation and comparison assistance
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
